notes: role is backed by generic live R5 V1 extended fields; semantic control naming remains unproven
candidate comes from an isolated role capture and is not a parser semantic mapping
candidate comes from an isolated role capture and is not a parser semantic mapping
input role semantics are diagnostic navigation only; they do not open HID or send output
generic_aux means the capture showed parser-visible R5 extended-field movement, but the role-specific semantic mapping remains unproven
semantic_candidates name observed R5 V1 extended slots from isolated captures only; they are not readiness claims
generic_aux evidence can keep passive artifacts valid while still marking input topology semantics as incomplete for product-quality follow-up

pit_house_compatibility.install_guidance: Install or update Pit House from the official MOZA Pit House Downloads support page; do not treat package-manager availability as authoritative evidence.
notes: Pit House compatibility is external smoke evidence only; it is not a native-control prerequisite.
Pit House install/source guidance is operator navigation only and does not prove coexistence.
Availability and case receipts are navigation evidence until pit-house-coexistence.json passes the verifier.
If Pit House is absent, keep open-state cases and smoke-ready blocked instead of fabricating evidence.
Simulator telemetry and bounded simulator FFB are smoke/external compatibility evidence; they are not native-control prerequisites.
Telemetry-only proof must remain no-output and cannot satisfy bounded FFB by itself.
Bounded simulator FFB can claim smoke evidence only after the telemetry gate and all safety prerequisites pass.

native_actuator_response_gate.details: native PIDFF actuator response smoke recorded 0.181 degree(s) of steering delta above the 0.100 degree response threshold; visible_success=Some(false)

zero_output_strategies.name: direct_report_0x20
notes: implemented by wheelctl moza zero
requires descriptor-derived output report 0x20 with the expected 8-byte shape

zero_output_strategies.name: pidff_device_control_stop_all
zero_output_strategies.description: standard HID PIDFF Device Control Stop All Effects zero-output candidate
notes: implemented by wheelctl moza zero --strategy pidff-stop-all
the candidate report carries a stop-all-effects command, not a force magnitude

notes: pre-output-readiness reads existing lane receipts only; it opens no HID device and sends no reports
ready_for_zero_torque is the first output-adjacent gate and still does not permit nonzero torque or FFB
ready_for_native_control is the OpenRacing-owned movement path and does not require SimHub, Pit House, or direct report 0x20
native_actuator_response_proven means bounded native PIDFF writes produced measured steering response above the response noise floor; it does not claim operator-visible motion
native_visible_motion_proven remains a stricter native claim and is false until an intentionally reviewed visible-motion profile passes
ready_for_external_compatibility tracks optional adapter/vendor-app surfaces such as simulator telemetry bridges and Pit House coexistence; it is not required for native control
ready_for_ffb is the legacy bounded simulator FFB preflight; keep it separate from native-control and release-readiness claims
input_role_semantics is diagnostic topology context; generic_aux mappings remain valid passive evidence but are not full role-specific semantic proof
